claim: Treat LLM workflows as ETL (Extract-Transform-Load) pipelines for unstructured text processing
actionable: Design your LLM systems to process documents through mapping operations (extract, transform) and reducing operations (aggregate, summarize) rather than one-shot prompt-based approaches.
source_title: AI Agents and LLM Judges at Scale: Processing Millions of Documents (Without Breaking the Bank)

claim: Error analysis on 50-100 human-reviewed traces is non-negotiable before deployment
actionable: Before deploying any LLM pipeline, manually review 50-100 outputs using open coding to identify failure modes, then iterate on your prompt/specification.
source_title: AI Agents and LLM Judges at Scale: Processing Millions of Documents (Without Breaking the Bank)

claim: Use gleaning to validate outputs: cheap LLM judges check properties (diversity, count) and trigger reruns until constraints are met
actionable: Define code-based or LLM-based validators for output properties; if validation fails, rerun the operation until it passes rather than accepting any output.
source_title: AI Agents and LLM Judges at Scale: Processing Millions of Documents (Without Breaking the Bank)

claim: LLM judges work best with structured rubrics and pairwise comparisons, not single-scale ratings
actionable: Define explicit rubrics for what you want to evaluate (precision, recall, etc.) and use pairwise comparisons (output A vs B) rather than single-pass ratings.
source_title: AI Agents and LLM Judges at Scale: Processing Millions of Documents (Without Breaking the Bank)

claim: Swap cheaper models for expensive ones only when the cheap model is highly confident or the task is simple
actionable: Use cheaper models (GPT-4 Mini, Claude Haiku) as fallbacks when confidence is high or task complexity is low; use expensive models (GPT-4, Claude Opus) for complex reasoning.
source_title: AI Agents and LLM Judges at Scale: Processing Millions of Documents (Without Breaking the Bank)

claim: Agents excel at rewriting and optimizing pipelines, but cannot replace manual error analysis
actionable: Use agents to help rewrite pipelines after you've done manual error analysis; do not skip the human error analysis step expecting agents to find your specification gaps.
source_title: AI Agents and LLM Judges at Scale: Processing Millions of Documents (Without Breaking the Bank)

claim: High-stakes applications (police misconduct database, legal documents) require multiple rounds of error analysis (3+ iterations)
actionable: For production systems with regulatory or ethical consequences, plan for 3+ error analysis cycles with human reviewers before deployment.
source_title: AI Agents and LLM Judges at Scale: Processing Millions of Documents (Without Breaking the Bank)

claim: Minimize LLM judge costs by using cheapest models, limiting to binary evaluation, and leveraging context caching
actionable: For LLM judges: use GPT-4 Mini or similar cheap model, ask binary questions (A vs B), and structure prompts to reuse cached context on subsequent calls.
source_title: AI Agents and LLM Judges at Scale: Processing Millions of Documents (Without Breaking the Bank)

claim: Clear output format specification in prompts prevents inconsistency errors (e.g., first_name, last_name required)
actionable: Always specify expected output format (JSON schema, field names, structure) in your LLM prompts to reduce variance in extraction tasks.
source_title: AI Agents and LLM Judges at Scale: Processing Millions of Documents (Without Breaking the Bank)
